file.content:
"""CLI entry point for the generic structural parser.

Usage:
    # Parse a single document
    python -m src.parser.parse_cli \
        --profile profiles/vzw_oa_profile.json \
        --doc data/extracted/LTEDATARETRY_ir.json \
        --output data/parsed/LTEDATARETRY_tree.json

    # Parse all documents in a directory
    python -m src.parser.parse_cli \
        --profile profiles/vzw_oa_profile.json \
        --docs-dir data/extracted \
        --output-dir data/parsed
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import logging
import time
from pathlib import Path

from src.models.document import DocumentIR
from src.profiler.profile_schema import DocumentProfile
from src.parser.structural_parser import GenericStructuralParser


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Parse extracted documents into requirement trees using a document profile."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--profile", required=True, type=Path,
        help="Path to document profile JSON",
    )

    group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
    group.add_argument("--doc", type=Path, help="Path to a single extracted IR JSON")
    group.add_argument("--docs-dir", type=Path, help="Directory of extracted IR JSONs")

    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, help="Output path (for single doc)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/parsed"),
        help="Output directory (for batch). Default: data/parsed",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", "-v", action="store_true")

    args = parser.parse_args()
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else logging.INFO,
        format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s",
    )

    profile = DocumentProfile.load_json(args.profile)
    structural_parser = GenericStructuralParser(profile)

    if args.doc:
        files = [args.doc]
        output_dir = args.output.parent if args.output else args.output_dir
    else:
        files = sorted(args.docs_dir.glob("*_ir.json"))
        output_dir = args.output_dir

    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    start = time.time()
    for f in files:
        t0 = time.time()
        doc = DocumentIR.load_json(f)
        tree = structural_parser.parse(doc)

        out_name = f.stem.replace("_ir", "_tree") + ".json"
        out_path = args.output if (args.output and len(files) == 1) else output_dir / out_name
        tree.save_json(out_path)

        elapsed = time.time() - t0
        logging.info(
            f"  {doc.source_file}: {len(tree.requirements)} requirements, "
            f"{elapsed:.1f}s -> {out_path}"
        )

    total = time.time() - start
    logging.info(f"\nParsed {len(files)} file(s) in {total:.1f}s")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
1	"""Generic, profile-driven structural parser (TDD 5.3).
2	
3	Parses normalized IR + document profile into a structured requirement
4	tree. No per-MNO code — behavior driven entirely by the profile JSON.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import json
10	import logging
11	import re
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
13	from pathlib import Path
14	from typing import Any
15	
16	from src.models.document import BlockType, ContentBlock, DocumentIR
17	from src.profiler.profile_schema import DocumentProfile, HeadingLevel
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20	
21	
22	# ── Output data structures ──────────────────────────────────────────
23	
24	
25	@dataclass
26	class TableData:
27	    headers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
28	    rows: list[list[str]] = field(default_factory=list)
29	    source: str = "inline"  # "inline" or "embedded_xlsx"
30	
31	
32	@dataclass
33	class ImageRef:
34	    path: str = ""
35	    surrounding_text: str = ""
36	
37	
38	@dataclass
39	class StandardsRef:
40	    spec: str = ""
41	    section: str = ""
42	    release: str = ""
43	
44	
45	@dataclass
46	class CrossReferences:
47	    internal: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # req IDs within same plan
48	    external_plans: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # other plan names
49	    standards: list[StandardsRef] = field(default_factory=list)
50	
51	
52	@dataclass
53	class Requirement:
54	    req_id: str = ""
55	    section_number: str = ""
56	    title: str = ""
57	    parent_req_id: str = ""
58	    parent_section: str = ""
59	    hierarchy_path: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
60	    zone_type: str = ""
61	    text: str = ""
62	    tables: list[TableData] = field(default_factory=list)
63	    images: list[ImageRef] = field(default_factory=list)
64	    children: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # child req_ids
65	    cross_references: CrossReferences = field(default_factory=CrossReferences)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass
69	class RequirementTree:
70	    mno: str = ""
71	    release: str = ""
72	    plan_id: str = ""
73	    plan_name: str = ""
74	    version: str = ""
75	    release_date: str = ""
76	    referenced_standards_releases: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
77	    requirements: list[Requirement] = field(default_factory=list)
78	
79	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
80	        return asdict(self)
81	
82	    def save_json(self, path: Path) -> None:
83	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
84	        with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
85	            json.dump(self.to_dict(), f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
86	
87	
88	# ── Parser ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
89	
90	
91	class GenericStructuralParser:
92	    """Profile-driven structural parser for requirement documents."""
93	
94	    def __init__(self, profile: DocumentProfile):
95	        self.profile = profile
96	        # Pre-compile regexes from profile
97	        self._num_re = (
98	            re.compile(profile.heading_detection.numbering_pattern)
99	            if profile.heading_detection.numbering_pattern
100	            else None
101	        )
102	        self._req_id_re = (
103	            re.compile(profile.requirement_id.pattern)
104	            if profile.requirement_id.pattern
105	            else None
106	        )
107	        # Cross-reference regexes
108	        self._std_res = [
109	            re.compile(p)
110	            for p in profile.cross_reference_patterns.standards_citations
111	        ]
112	        self._section_ref_re = (
113	            re.compile(profile.cross_reference_patterns.internal_section_refs)
114	            if profile.cross_reference_patterns.internal_section_refs
115	            else None
116	        )
117	        # 3GPP spec + section extraction (more specific than profile patterns)
118	        self._std_detail_re = re.compile(
119	            r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)\s+(?:[Ss]ection\s+)?([\d.]+)"
120	        )
121	        self._std_release_re = re.compile(
122	            r"3GPP\s+(?:TS\s+[\d.]+\s+)?[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)"
123	        )
124	        # Zone classification
125	        self._zone_map = {
126	            z.section_pattern: z.zone_type
127	            for z in profile.document_zones
128	        }
129	
130	    def parse(self, doc: DocumentIR) -> RequirementTree:
131	        """Parse a document IR into a structured requirement tree."""
132	        logger.info(f"Parsing {doc.source_file} with profile '{self.profile.profile_name}'")
133	
134	        # 1. Extract plan metadata
135	        plan_meta = self._extract_plan_metadata(doc)
136	
137	        # 2. Classify blocks and build section hierarchy
138	        sections = self._build_sections(doc)
139	
140	        # 3. Extract referenced standards releases
141	        std_releases = self._extract_standards_releases(doc)
142	
143	        # 4. Extract cross-references for each section
144	        plan_id = plan_meta.get("plan_id", "")
145	        for sec in sections:
146	            sec.cross_references = self._extract_cross_refs(sec.text, plan_id)
147	
148	        # 5. Build parent-child relationships
149	        self._link_parents(sections)
150	
151	        tree = RequirementTree(
152	            mno=doc.mno,
153	            release=doc.release,
154	            plan_id=plan_meta.get("plan_id", ""),
155	            plan_name=plan_meta.get("plan_name", ""),
156	            version=plan_meta.get("version", ""),
157	            release_date=plan_meta.get("release_date", ""),
158	            referenced_standards_releases=std_releases,
159	            requirements=sections,
160	        )
161	
162	        logger.info(
163	            f"Parsed {doc.source_file}: {len(sections)} requirements, "
164	            f"plan_id={tree.plan_id}"
165	        )
166	        return tree
167	
168	    # ── Plan metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────
169	
170	    def _extract_plan_metadata(self, doc: DocumentIR) -> dict[str, str]:
171	        """Extract plan-level metadata using profile patterns."""
172	        first_page_text = " ".join(
173	            b.text
174	            for b in doc.blocks_by_type(BlockType.PARAGRAPH)
175	            if b.position.page == 1
176	        )
177	
178	        meta = {}
179	        for field_name in ["plan_name", "plan_id", "version", "release_date"]:
180	            mf = getattr(self.profile.plan_metadata, field_name)
181	            if mf.pattern:
182	                m = re.search(mf.pattern, first_page_text)
183	                if m:
184	                    meta[field_name] = m.group(1).strip()
185	
186	        logger.info(f"Plan metadata: {meta}")
187	        return meta
188	
189	    # ── Section hierarchy ───────────────────────────────────────────
190	
191	    def _build_sections(self, doc: DocumentIR) -> list[Requirement]:
192	        """Build the flat list of sections with hierarchy info from content blocks."""
193	        sections: list[Requirement] = []
194	        current_section: Requirement | None = None
195	
196	        # Pending req ID — small font blocks that appear before/after a heading
197	        pending_req_id: str = ""
198	
199	        for block in doc.content_blocks:
200	            if block.type == BlockType.PARAGRAPH:
201	                if not block.font_info:
202	                    if current_section:
203	                        self._append_text(current_section, block.text)
204	                    continue
205	
206	                # Check if this is a requirement ID block (small font)
207	                if self._is_req_id_block(block):
208	                    req_ids = self._req_id_re.findall(block.text) if self._req_id_re else []
209	                    if req_ids:
210	                        # If we have a current section without a req_id, assign it
211	                        if current_section and not current_section.req_id:
212	                            current_section.req_id = req_ids[0]
213	                        else:
214	                            pending_req_id = req_ids[0]
215	                    continue
216	
217	                # Check if this is a heading block
218	                section_num, heading_text = self._classify_heading(block)
219	                if section_num:
220	                    # New section
221	                    current_section = Requirement(
222	                        section_number=section_num,
223	                        title=heading_text,
224	                        req_id=pending_req_id,
225	                        zone_type=self._classify_zone(section_num),
226	                    )
227	                    pending_req_id = ""
228	                    sections.append(current_section)
229	                    continue
230	
231	                # Body text — append to current section
232	                if current_section:
233	                    self._append_text(current_section, block.text)
234	                    # Also check for inline req IDs in body text
235	                    if self._req_id_re and not current_section.req_id:
236	                        ids = self._req_id_re.findall(block.text)
237	                        if ids:
238	                            current_section.req_id = ids[0]
239	
240	            elif block.type == BlockType.TABLE:
241	                if current_section:
242	                    current_section.tables.append(
243	                        TableData(
244	                            headers=block.headers,
245	                            rows=block.rows,
246	                            source="inline",
247	                        )
248	                    )
249	
250	            elif block.type == BlockType.IMAGE:
251	                if current_section:
252	                    current_section.images.append(
253	                        ImageRef(
254	                            path=block.image_path,
255	                            surrounding_text=block.surrounding_text,
256	                        )
257	                    )
258	
259	        return sections
260	
261	    def _is_req_id_block(self, block: ContentBlock) -> bool:
262	        """Check if a block is a standalone requirement ID (small font)."""
263	        if not block.font_info or not self._req_id_re:
264	            return False
265	        # Req IDs in VZW docs appear at small font (7pt), distinct from
266	        # body text (12pt) and headings (14pt)
267	        body_mid = (
268	            self.profile.body_text.font_size_min
269	            + self.profile.body_text.font_size_max
270	        ) / 2
271	        if block.font_info.size < body_mid - 2.0:
272	            return bool(self._req_id_re.search(block.text))
273	        return False
274	
275	    def _classify_heading(
276	        self, block: ContentBlock
277	    ) -> tuple[str, str]:
278	        """Check if a block is a heading. Returns (section_number, title) or ("", "")."""
279	        if not block.font_info:
280	            return "", ""
281	
282	        # Check against profile heading levels
283	        is_heading_font = False
284	        for lv in self.profile.heading_detection.levels:
285	            if lv.font_size_min <= block.font_info.size <= lv.font_size_max:
286	                if lv.bold is not None and block.font_info.bold != lv.bold:
287	                    continue
288	                is_heading_font = True
289	                break
290	
291	        if not is_heading_font:
292	            return "", ""
293	
294	        # Must have a section number to be treated as a structural heading
295	        if not self._num_re:
296	            return "", ""
297	
298	        text = block.text.strip()
299	        m = self._num_re.match(text)
300	        if not m:
301	            return "", ""
302	
303	        section_num = m.group(0).strip().rstrip(".")
304	        # Clean: ensure consistent format (remove trailing dots)
305	        section_num = re.sub(r"\.$", "", section_num)
306	        title = text[m.end():].strip()
307	
308	        return section_num, title
309	
310	    def _classify_zone(self, section_number: str) -> str:
311	        """Classify a section into a document zone using profile rules."""
312	        for pattern, zone_type in self._zone_map.items():
313	            if re.match(pattern, section_number):
314	                return zone_type
315	        return ""
316	
317	    @staticmethod
318	    def _append_text(section: Requirement, text: str) -> None:
319	        """Append text to a section, with paragraph separation."""
320	        text = text.strip()
321	        if not text:
322	            return
323	        if section.text:
324	            section.text += "\n" + text
325	        else:
326	            section.text = text
327	
328	    # ── Parent-child linking ────────────────────────────────────────
329	
330	    def _link_parents(self, sections: list[Requirement]) -> None:
331	        """Build parent-child relationships and hierarchy paths."""
332	        # Build lookup: section_number → Requirement
333	        by_section: dict[str, Requirement] = {}
334	        for sec in sections:
335	            by_section[sec.section_number] = sec
336	
337	        for sec in sections:
338	            if not sec.section_number:
339	                continue
340	
341	            # Find parent section number by trimming the last component
342	            parts = sec.section_number.split(".")
343	            if len(parts) > 1:
344	                parent_num = ".".join(parts[:-1])
345	                parent = by_section.get(parent_num)
346	                if parent:
347	                    sec.parent_section = parent_num
348	                    sec.parent_req_id = parent.req_id
349	                    if sec.req_id not in parent.children:
350	                        parent.children.append(sec.req_id or sec.section_number)
351	
352	            # Build hierarchy path
353	            sec.hierarchy_path = self._build_hierarchy_path(
354	                sec.section_number, by_section
355	            )
356	
357	    @staticmethod
358	    def _build_hierarchy_path(
359	        section_number: str,
360	        by_section: dict[str, Requirement],
361	    ) -> list[str]:
362	        """Build the hierarchy path from root to this section."""
363	        parts = section_number.split(".")
364	        path = []
365	        for i in range(1, len(parts) + 1):
366	            ancestor_num = ".".join(parts[:i])
367	            ancestor = by_section.get(ancestor_num)
368	            if ancestor:
369	                path.append(ancestor.title or ancestor_num)
370	        return path
371	
372	    # ── Cross-reference extraction ──────────────────────────────────
373	
374	    def _extract_cross_refs(
375	        self, text: str, own_plan_id: str
376	    ) -> CrossReferences:
377	        """Extract cross-references from section text."""
378	        if not text:
379	            return CrossReferences()
380	
381	        refs = CrossReferences()
382	
383	        # Internal requirement ID references
384	        if self._req_id_re:
385	            for rid in self._req_id_re.findall(text):
386	                # Check if it references another plan
387	                m = re.match(r"VZ_REQ_([A-Z0-9]+)_\d+", rid)
388	                if m:
389	                    ref_plan = m.group(1)
390	                    if ref_plan != own_plan_id:
391	                        if ref_plan not in refs.external_plans:
392	                            refs.external_plans.append(ref_plan)
393	                    else:
394	                        if rid not in refs.internal:
395	                            refs.internal.append(rid)
396	
397	        # Standards references — with section numbers
398	        seen_std: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
399	        for m in self._std_detail_re.finditer(text):
400	            spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(1)}"
401	            section = m.group(2)
402	            key = (spec, section)
403	            if key in seen_std:
404	                continue
405	            seen_std.add(key)
406	            # Try to find release nearby
407	            release = ""
408	            context = text[max(0, m.start() - 100):m.end() + 100]
409	            rm = self._std_release_re.search(context)
410	            if rm:
411	                release = f"Release {rm.group(1)}"
412	            refs.standards.append(
413	                StandardsRef(spec=spec, section=section, release=release)
414	            )
415	
416	        # Standards references — spec only (no section number)
417	        spec_only_re = re.compile(r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)")
418	        for m in spec_only_re.finditer(text):
419	            spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(1)}"
420	            if not any(s.spec == spec for s in refs.standards):
421	                release = ""
422	                context = text[max(0, m.start() - 100):m.end() + 100]
423	                rm = self._std_release_re.search(context)
424	                if rm:
425	                    release = f"Release {rm.group(1)}"
426	                refs.standards.append(
427	                    StandardsRef(spec=spec, section="", release=release)
428	                )
429	
430	        return refs
431	
432	    # ── Standards releases ──────────────────────────────────────────
433	
434	    def _extract_standards_releases(
435	        self, doc: DocumentIR
436	    ) -> dict[str, str]:
437	        """Extract referenced standards releases from the document.
438	
439	        Scans all text for patterns like:
440	        - "3GPP TS 24.301 Release 10"
441	        - "Release 10 version of 3GPP TS 24.301"
442	        """
443	        releases: dict[str, str] = {}
444	
445	        # Pattern 1: "3GPP TS X.X ... Release N"
446	        pat1 = re.compile(r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+).*?[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)")
447	        # Pattern 2: "Release N ... 3GPP TS X.X" (VZW style)
448	        pat2 = re.compile(r"[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)\s+(?:version\s+of\s+)?3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)")
449	
450	        for b in doc.content_blocks:
451	            for m in pat1.finditer(b.text):
452	                spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(1)}"
453	                release = f"Release {m.group(2)}"
454	                if spec not in releases:
455	                    releases[spec] = release
456	            for m in pat2.finditer(b.text):
457	                spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(2)}"
458	                release = f"Release {m.group(1)}"
459	                if spec not in releases:
460	                    releases[spec] = release
461	
462	        return releases

content.text:
Good, I've read through the entire codebase. Let me give you a straight review — things that are actually broken, things that are architecturally concerning, and things that are fine for PoC stage.

---

## Code Review: PoC Steps 1-3

### Bugs (need fixing)

**1. Resource leak in `PDFExtractor.extract()`** (`pdf_extractor.py:56-57`)

`fitz_doc` and `plumber_pdf` are opened but not wrapped in try/finally or context managers. Any exception during extraction leaks file handles.

**2. Trailing dot in 3GPP spec numbers** (visible in parsed output)

The regex `r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)"` greedily captures trailing dots from sentence punctuation. Your output already shows `"3GPP TS 24.301."` as a key (with trailing dot) vs `"3GPP TS 24.301"` (without). Two entries for the same spec.

**3. Hardcoded `VZ_REQ` breaks the "generic" promise** 

- `profiler.py:629`: `re.findall(r"VZ_REQ_[A-Z0-9_]+_\d+", ...)` — hardcoded instead of using the detected pattern
- `structural_parser.py:387`: `re.match(r"VZ_REQ_([A-Z0-9]+)_\d+", rid)` — same
- `structural_parser.py:417-428`: hardcoded `3GPP TS` spec-only regex

These defeat the profile-driven design. If you feed TMO or ATT docs, cross-reference extraction silently produces empty results.

**4. `SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS` mismatch** (`extract.py:24` vs `registry.py:13-15`)

The CLI advertises `.doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx` support but the registry only has `.pdf`. Users will get files collected then a confusing `ValueError` from the registry.

**5. Profiler header/footer detection is a no-op** (`profiler.py:543-587`)

The profiler's `_detect_header_footer` analyzes IR blocks — but the PDFExtractor already stripped headers/footers before producing the IR. The profiler will never see them. This explains why `header_footer.header_patterns` and `footer_patterns` are empty in the output. The detection needs to happen at the extractor level (which it does — `_detect_header_footer_patterns`), but the profiler duplicates the concept without access to the raw data.

### Design Concerns

**6. Heading levels are misleading**

The profile output shows three "levels" all at 13.5-14.5pt, differentiated only by bold/caps. But these don't map to section depth — section 1.1 and 1.3.1.3.16 can have the same font attributes. The parser correctly ignores heading "level" and uses section numbering for hierarchy, so this isn't a functional bug. But the profile data structure implies level = depth, and anyone editing the profile would be misled.

The real heading classification is binary: "is this font size a heading?" Yes/no. The depth comes from the section number.

**7. `update_profile` is incomplete** (`profiler.py:86-129`)

It updates req IDs, cross-refs, and zones, but doesn't touch heading levels, body text detection, or plan metadata patterns. If a new representative doc reveals different font sizes, the profile stays stale.

**8. No tests**

Zero test files. For a PoC processing real documents, you need at least:
- Unit tests for the data model round-trip (serialize → deserialize)
- A smoke test for the extraction → profile → parse pipeline on a known PDF
- Tests for the regex patterns (section numbering, req IDs, cross-refs)

Without these, every change to any of the three steps risks silently breaking downstream.

**9. Dead code: `metadata` dict in PDFExtractor** (`pdf_extractor.py:157`)

`metadata: dict = {}` is created for every text block but never populated. Either use it or remove it.

### Minor / PoC-acceptable

- **ContentBlock kitchen-sink dataclass** — not great long-term but fine for PoC since you have a single representation flowing through the pipeline.
- **O(n) `_get_surrounding_text`** — scans all blocks backwards per image. Won't matter at this scale.
- **Req ID tables captured as data tables** — section 1.1 in the output has a "table" that's just a requirement ID. The parser doesn't distinguish structural tables from these formatting artifacts. Worth noting for later.

---

### What I'd recommend fixing now

Items 1-5 are actual bugs. Want me to fix them? Here's my priority order:

1. **Resource leak** — simple fix, high risk if left
2. **Hardcoded VZ_REQ** — directly undermines the architecture's selling point
3. **Trailing dot regex** — data quality bug in output
4. **SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS mismatch** — user-facing confusion
5. **Profiler header/footer** — remove the dead duplication from profiler, keep the extractor's version

Items 6-9 I'd hold for a follow-up unless you want to tackle them now.

What's your call?

**Fix 1: Resource leak in PDFExtractor**

fitz_doc.close()
            raise
        try:
            return self._extract_impl(file_path, fitz_doc, plumber_pdf, mno, release, doc_type)
        finally:
            fitz_doc.close()
            plumber_pdf.close()

        # First pass: detect repeating header/footer text across pages
        header_footer_patterns = self._detect_header_footer_patterns(fitz_doc)
        logger.info(
            f"Detected {len(header_footer_patterns)} header/footer patterns"
        )

        all_blocks: list[ContentBlock] = []
        images_dir = (
            file_path.parent / "extracted_images" / file_path.stem
        )

        for page_num in range(len(fitz_doc)):
            page = fitz_doc[page_num]
            plumber_page = plumber_pdf.pages[page_num]
            page_height = page.rect.height

            # --- Tables (pdfplumber) ---
            table_bboxes: list[tuple[float, float, float, float]] = []
            plumber_tables = plumber_page.find_tables()
            for table_obj in plumber_tables:
                bbox = table_obj.bbox  # (x0, y0, x1, y1) top-left origin
                # pdfplumber uses top-left origin, same as our convention
                table_bboxes.append(bbox)
                table_data = table_obj.extract()
                if not table_data or len(table_data) < 1:
                    continue
                headers = [
                    str(c).strip() if c else "" for c in table_data[0]
                ]
                rows = [
                    [str(c).strip() if c else "" for c in row]
                    for row in table_data[1:]
                ]
                # Skip degenerate tables: single column with empty or near-empty content
                non_empty_headers = [h for h in headers if h]
                total_cells = sum(1 for row in rows for c in row if c)
                if len(non_empty_headers) <= 1 and total_cells == 0:
                    continue
                all_blocks.append(
                    ContentBlock(
                        type=BlockType.TABLE,
                        position=Position(
                            page=page_num + 1,
                            index=0,  # assigned later
                            bbox=bbox,
                        ),
                        headers=headers,
                        rows=rows,
                    )
                )

            # --- Text blocks (pymupdf) ---
            fitz_blocks = page.get_text("dict", flags=fitz.TEXT_PRESERVE_WHITESPACE)[
                "blocks"
            ]
            for fb in fitz_blocks:
                if fb["type"] != 0:  # skip image blocks (handled below)
                    continue

                bbox = (fb["bbox"][0], fb["bbox"][1], fb["bbox"][2], fb["bbox"][3])

                # Skip tiny blocks
                width = bbox[2] - bbox[0]
                height = bbox[3] - bbox[1]
                if width * height < self.MIN_BLOCK_AREA:
                    continue

                # Skip header/footer regions
                if self._is_in_margin(bbox, page_height):
                    continue

                # Skip blocks that overlap with detected tables
                if self._overlaps_any_table(bbox, table_bboxes):
                    continue

                # Process spans into content blocks
                text_segments = self._extract_text_segments(fb)

                # Skip if this matches a header/footer pattern
                full_text = " ".join(seg["text"] for seg in text_segments)
                if self._matches_header_footer(full_text, header_footer_patterns):
                    continue
                if self.PAGE_NUMBER_RE.match(full_text):
                    continue
                if self.CONFIDENTIAL_RE.search(full_text):
                    continue

                if not text_segments:
                    continue

                # Group segments by font characteristics to split mixed-font blocks
                groups = self._group_by_font(text_segments)
                for group in groups:
                    text = group["text"].strip()
                    if not text:
                        continue
                    font = group["font_info"]
                    block_type = BlockType.PARAGRAPH
                    metadata: dict = {}

                    all_blocks.append(
                        ContentBlock(
                            type=block_type,
                            position=Position(
                                page=page_num + 1,
                                index=0,
                                bbox=bbox,
                            ),
                            text=text,
                            font_info=font,
                            metadata=metadata,
                        )
                    )

            # --- Images (pymupdf) ---
            for img_idx, img_info in enumerate(page.get_images()):
                xref = img_info[0]
                try:
                    base_image = fitz_doc.extract_image(xref)
                    if not base_image or base_image["width"] < 20 or base_image["height"] < 20:
                        continue  # skip tiny images (likely decorative)

                    img_ext = base_image["ext"]
                    img_filename = f"p{page_num + 1}_{img_idx:03d}.{img_ext}"
                    img_path = images_dir / img_filename

                    img_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    with open(img_path, "wb") as f:
                        f.write(base_image["image"])

                    # Get surrounding text for context
                    surrounding = self._get_surrounding_text(
                        all_blocks, page_num + 1
                    )

                    all_blocks.append(
                        ContentBlock(
                            type=BlockType.IMAGE,
                            position=Position(
                                page=page_num + 1,
                                index=0,
                                bbox=None,
                            ),
                            image_path=str(img_path.relative_to(file_path.parent)),
                            surrounding_text=surrounding,
                        )
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(
                        f"Failed to extract image xref={xref} on page {page_num + 1}: {e}"
                    )

        total_pages = len(fitz_doc)
        fitz_doc.close()
        plumber_pdf.close()

        # Sort by page, then vertical position (y0 of bbox)
        all_blocks.sort(
            key=lambda b: (
                b.position.page,
                b.position.bbox[1] if b.position.bbox else 9999,
            )
        )

        # Assign sequential indices
        for i, block in enumerate(all_blocks):
            block.position.index = i

        doc_ir = DocumentIR(
            source_file=file_path.name,
            source_format="pdf",
            mno=mno,
            release=release,
            doc_type=doc_type,
            content_blocks=all_blocks,
            extraction_metadata={
                "page_count": total_pages,
                "header_footer_patterns": header_footer_patterns,
                "images_dir": str(images_dir.relative_to(file_path.parent))
                if images_dir.exists()
                else None,
            },
        )

        logger.info(
            f"Extracted {file_path.name}: {doc_ir.block_count} blocks "
            f"({len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.PARAGRAPH))} text, "
            f"{len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.TABLE))} tables, "
            f"{len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.IMAGE))} images)"
        )

        return doc_ir

def _extract_impl(
        self,
        file_path: Path,
        fitz_doc: fitz.Document,
        plumber_pdf: pdfplumber.PDF,
        mno: str,
        release: str,
        doc_type: str,
    ) -> DocumentIR:
        # First pass: detect repeating header/footer text across pages
        header_footer_patterns = self._detect_header_footer_patterns(fitz_doc)
        logger.info(
            f"Detected {len(header_footer_patterns)} header/footer patterns"
        )

        all_blocks: list[ContentBlock] = []
        images_dir = (
            file_path.parent / "extracted_images" / file_path.stem
        )

        for page_num in range(len(fitz_doc)):
            page = fitz_doc[page_num]
            plumber_page = plumber_pdf.pages[page_num]
            page_height = page.rect.height

            # --- Tables (pdfplumber) ---
            table_bboxes: list[tuple[float, float, float, float]] = []
            plumber_tables = plumber_page.find_tables()
            for table_obj in plumber_tables:
                bbox = table_obj.bbox  # (x0, y0, x1, y1) top-left origin
                # pdfplumber uses top-left origin, same as our convention
                table_bboxes.append(bbox)
                table_data = table_obj.extract()
                if not table_data or len(table_data) < 1:
                    continue
                headers = [
                    str(c).strip() if c else "" for c in table_data[0]
                ]
                rows = [
                    [str(c).strip() if c else "" for c in row]
                    for row in table_data[1:]
                ]
                # Skip degenerate tables: single column with empty or near-empty content
                non_empty_headers = [h for h in headers if h]
                total_cells = sum(1 for row in rows for c in row if c)
                if len(non_empty_headers) <= 1 and total_cells == 0:
                    continue
                all_blocks.append(
                    ContentBlock(
                        type=BlockType.TABLE,
                        position=Position(
                            page=page_num + 1,
                            index=0,  # assigned later
                            bbox=bbox,
                        ),
                        headers=headers,
                        rows=rows,
                    )
                )

            # --- Text blocks (pymupdf) ---
            fitz_blocks = page.get_text("dict", flags=fitz.TEXT_PRESERVE_WHITESPACE)[
                "blocks"
            ]
            for fb in fitz_blocks:
                if fb["type"] != 0:  # skip image blocks (handled below)
                    continue

                bbox = (fb["bbox"][0], fb["bbox"][1], fb["bbox"][2], fb["bbox"][3])

                # Skip tiny blocks
                width = bbox[2] - bbox[0]
                height = bbox[3] - bbox[1]
                if width * height < self.MIN_BLOCK_AREA:
                    continue

                # Skip header/footer regions
                if self._is_in_margin(bbox, page_height):
                    continue

                # Skip blocks that overlap with detected tables
                if self._overlaps_any_table(bbox, table_bboxes):
                    continue

                # Process spans into content blocks
                text_segments = self._extract_text_segments(fb)

                # Skip if this matches a header/footer pattern
                full_text = " ".join(seg["text"] for seg in text_segments)
                if self._matches_header_footer(full_text, header_footer_patterns):
                    continue
                if self.PAGE_NUMBER_RE.match(full_text):
                    continue
                if self.CONFIDENTIAL_RE.search(full_text):
                    continue

                if not text_segments:
                    continue

                # Group segments by font characteristics to split mixed-font blocks
                groups = self._group_by_font(text_segments)
                for group in groups:
                    text = group["text"].strip()
                    if not text:
                        continue
                    font = group["font_info"]

                    all_blocks.append(
                        ContentBlock(
                            type=BlockType.PARAGRAPH,
                            position=Position(
                                page=page_num + 1,
                                index=0,
                                bbox=bbox,
                            ),
                            text=text,
                            font_info=font,
                        )
                    )

            # --- Images (pymupdf) ---
            for img_idx, img_info in enumerate(page.get_images()):
                xref = img_info[0]
                try:
                    base_image = fitz_doc.extract_image(xref)
                    if not base_image or base_image["width"] < 20 or base_image["height"] < 20:
                        continue  # skip tiny images (likely decorative)

                    img_ext = base_image["ext"]
                    img_filename = f"p{page_num + 1}_{img_idx:03d}.{img_ext}"
                    img_path = images_dir / img_filename

                    img_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    with open(img_path, "wb") as f:
                        f.write(base_image["image"])

                    # Get surrounding text for context
                    surrounding = self._get_surrounding_text(
                        all_blocks, page_num + 1
                    )

                    all_blocks.append(
                        ContentBlock(
                            type=BlockType.IMAGE,
                            position=Position(
                                page=page_num + 1,
                                index=0,
                                bbox=None,
                            ),
                            image_path=str(img_path.relative_to(file_path.parent)),
                            surrounding_text=surrounding,
                        )
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(
                        f"Failed to extract image xref={xref} on page {page_num + 1}: {e}"
                    )

        total_pages = len(fitz_doc)

        # Sort by page, then vertical position (y0 of bbox)
        all_blocks.sort(
            key=lambda b: (
                b.position.page,
                b.position.bbox[1] if b.position.bbox else 9999,
            )
        )

        # Assign sequential indices
        for i, block in enumerate(all_blocks):
            block.position.index = i

        doc_ir = DocumentIR(
            source_file=file_path.name,
            source_format="pdf",
            mno=mno,
            release=release,
            doc_type=doc_type,
            content_blocks=all_blocks,
            extraction_metadata={
                "page_count": total_pages,
                "header_footer_patterns": header_footer_patterns,
                "images_dir": str(images_dir.relative_to(file_path.parent))
                if images_dir.exists()
                else None,
            },
        )

        logger.info(
            f"Extracted {file_path.name}: {doc_ir.block_count} blocks "
            f"({len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.PARAGRAPH))} text, "
            f"{len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.TABLE))} tables, "
            f"{len(doc_ir.blocks_by_type(BlockType.IMAGE))} images)"
        )

        return doc_ir

589	    def _detect_cross_references(
590	        self,
591	        docs: list[DocumentIR],
592	    ) -> CrossReferencePatterns:
593	        """Detect cross-reference patterns in document text."""
594	        # Check for various standards citation formats
595	        standards_patterns = [
596	            r"3GPP\s+TS\s+[\d.]+(?:\s+[Ss]ection\s+[\d.]+)?",
597	            r"3GPP\s+TS\s+[\d.]+(?:\s+[Rr]elease\s+\d+)?",
598	            r"GSMA\s+\w+[\d.]*",
599	            r"OMA\s+\w+[\d.]*",
600	        ]
601	
602	        found_patterns: list[str] = []
603	        for pattern in standards_patterns:
604	            regex = re.compile(pattern)
605	            total_matches = 0
606	            for doc in docs:
607	                for b in doc.content_blocks:
608	                    total_matches += len(regex.findall(b.text))
609	            if total_matches > 0:
610	                found_patterns.append(pattern)
611	                logger.info(
612	                    f"Cross-ref pattern '{pattern}': {total_matches} matches"
613	                )
614	
615	        # Internal section references
616	        section_ref_pattern = r"[Ss]ee\s+[Ss]ection\s+[\d.]+"
617	        section_ref_count = 0
618	        for doc in docs:
619	            for b in doc.content_blocks:
620	                section_ref_count += len(
621	                    re.findall(section_ref_pattern, b.text)
622	                )
623	
624	        # Requirement ID references (reuse the detected pattern)
625	        req_pattern = ""
626	        req_count = 0
627	        for doc in docs:
628	            for b in doc.content_blocks:
629	                found = re.findall(r"VZ_REQ_[A-Z0-9_]+_\d+", b.text)
630	                req_count += len(found)
631	        if req_count > 0:
632	            req_pattern = r"VZ_REQ_[A-Z0-9_]+_\d+"
633	
634	        return CrossReferencePatterns(
635	            standards_citations=found_patterns,
636	            internal_section_refs=section_ref_pattern
637	            if section_ref_count > 0
638	            else "",
639	            requirement_id_refs=req_pattern,
640	        )
641	
642	    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
643	    # Helpers

def create_profile(
        self,
        docs: list[DocumentIR],
        profile_name: str = "",
    ) -> DocumentProfile:
        """Create a new profile from representative documents."""
        logger.info(
            f"Creating profile from {len(docs)} document(s): "
            f"{[d.source_file for d in docs]}"
        )

        all_text_blocks = []
        for doc in docs:
            all_text_blocks.extend(doc.blocks_by_type(BlockType.PARAGRAPH))

        body = self._detect_body_text(all_text_blocks)
        heading = self._detect_headings(all_text_blocks, body)
        req_id = self._detect_requirement_ids(docs)
        plan_meta = self._detect_plan_metadata(docs)
        zones = self._detect_document_zones(docs, heading)
        hf = self._detect_header_footer(docs)
        xrefs = self._detect_cross_references(docs)

        profile = DocumentProfile(
            profile_name=profile_name or self._derive_profile_name(docs),
            profile_version=1,
            created_from=[d.source_file for d in docs],
            last_updated=date.today().isoformat(),
            heading_detection=heading,
            requirement_id=req_id,
            plan_metadata=plan_meta,
            document_zones=zones,
            header_footer=hf,
            cross_reference_patterns=xrefs,
            body_text=body,
        )

        self._log_profile_summary(profile)
        return profile

input.old_string: xrefs = self._detect_cross_references(docs)

file.content:
class GenericStructuralParser:
    """Profile-driven structural parser for requirement documents."""

    def __init__(self, profile: DocumentProfile):
        self.profile = profile
        # Pre-compile regexes from profile
        self._num_re = (
            re.compile(profile.heading_detection.numbering_pattern)
            if profile.heading_detection.numbering_pattern
            else None
        )
        self._req_id_re = (
            re.compile(profile.requirement_id.pattern)
            if profile.requirement_id.pattern
            else None
        )
        # Cross-reference regexes
        self._std_res = [
            re.compile(p)
            for p in profile.cross_reference_patterns.standards_citations
        ]
        self._section_ref_re = (
            re.compile(profile.cross_reference_patterns.internal_section_refs)
            if profile.cross_reference_patterns.internal_section_refs
            else None
        )
        # 3GPP spec + section extraction (more specific than profile patterns)
        self._std_detail_re = re.compile(
            r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)\s+(?:[Ss]ection\s+)?([\d.]+)"
        )
        self._std_release_re = re.compile(
            r"3GPP\s+(?:TS\s+[\d.]+\s+)?[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)"
        )
        # Zone classification
        self._zone_map = {
            z.section_pattern: z.zone_type
            for z in profile.document_zones
        }

377	        """Extract cross-references from section text."""

382

386	                # Check if it references another plan
387	                m = re.match(r"VZ_REQ_([A-Z0-9]+)_\d+", rid)
388	                if m:
389	                    ref_plan = m.group(1)
390	                    if ref_plan != own_plan_id:
391	                        if ref_plan not in refs.external_plans:
392	                            refs.external_plans.append(ref_plan)
393	                    else:
394	                        if rid not in refs.internal:
395	                            refs.internal.append(rid)
396

398	        seen_std: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
399	        for m in self._std_detail_re.finditer(text):
400	            spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(1)}"
401	            section = m.group(2)
402	            key = (spec, section)
403	            if key in seen_std:
404	                continue
405	            seen_std.add(key)
406	            # Try to find release nearby
407	            release = ""
408	            context = text[max(0, m.start() - 100):m.end() + 100]
409	            rm = self._std_release_re.search(context)
410	            if rm:
411	                release = f"Release {rm.group(1)}"
412	            refs.standards.append(
413	                StandardsRef(spec=spec, section=section, release=release)
414	            )
415	
416	        # Standards references — spec only (no section number)

421	                release = ""

424	                if rm:
425	                    release = f"Release {rm.group(1)}"
426	                refs.standards.append(
427	                    StandardsRef(spec=spec, section="", release=release)
428	                )
429	
430	        return refs

self, doc: DocumentIR
    ) -> dict[str, str]:
        """Extract referenced standards releases from the document.

        Scans all text for patterns like:
        - "3GPP TS 24.301 Release 10"
        - "Release 10 version of 3GPP TS 24.301"
        """
        releases: dict[str, str] = {}

        # Pattern 1: "3GPP TS X.X ... Release N"
        pat1 = re.compile(r"3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+).*?[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)")
        # Pattern 2: "Release N ... 3GPP TS X.X" (VZW style)
        pat2 = re.compile(r"[Rr]elease\s+(\d+)\s+(?:version\s+of\s+)?3GPP\s+TS\s+([\d.]+)")

        for b in doc.content_blocks:
            for m in pat1.finditer(b.text):
                spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(1)}"
                release = f"Release {m.group(2)}"
                if spec not in releases:
                    releases[spec] = release
            for m in pat2.finditer(b.text):
                spec = f"3GPP TS {m.group(2)}"
                release = f"Release {m.group(1)}"
                if spec not in releases:
                    releases[spec] = release

        return releases

content.text: The right fix here is to make the CLI derive supported extensions from the registry instead of maintaining a second list. That way when you add a DOCX extractor later, it works everywhere automatically.
I'll expose the supported extensions from the registry:

toolUseResult.oldString: def get_extractor(file_path: Path) -> BaseExtractor:
toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Extractor registry — maps file extensions to format-specific extractors."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from src.extraction.base import BaseExtractor
from src.extraction.pdf_extractor import PDFExtractor
from src.models.document import DocumentIR


# Extractor instances, keyed by file extension
_EXTRACTORS: dict[str, BaseExtractor] = {
    ".pdf": PDFExtractor(),
}


def get_extractor(file_path: Path) -> BaseExtractor:
    """Get the appropriate extractor for a file based on its extension."""
    ext = file_path.suffix.lower()
    if ext not in _EXTRACTORS:
        supported = ", ".join(sorted(_EXTRACTORS.keys()))
        raise ValueError(
            f"No extractor for '{ext}' files. Supported: {supported}"
        )
    return _EXTRACTORS[ext]


def extract_document(
    file_path: Path,
    mno: str = "",
    release: str = "",
    doc_type: str = "",
) -> DocumentIR:
    """Extract a document using the appropriate format extractor."""
    extractor = get_extractor(file_path)
    return extractor.extract(file_path, mno=mno, release=release, doc_type=doc_type)


def infer_metadata_from_path(
    file_path: Path,
) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Infer mno, release, and doc_type from folder structure.

    Expected structure: .../<mno>/<release>/<doc_type>/filename.ext
    e.g., data/raw/vzw/2026_feb/requirements/LTEDATARETRY.pdf
    """
    parts = file_path.resolve().parts
    metadata = {"mno": "", "release": "", "doc_type": ""}

    # Walk backwards from the file to find the expected structure
    # filename -> doc_type_folder -> release_folder -> mno_folder
    if len(parts) >= 4:
        doc_type_folder = parts[-2].lower()
        if doc_type_folder in ("requirements", "testcases"):
            metadata["doc_type"] = (
                "requirement" if doc_type_folder == "requirements" else "testcase"
            )
            metadata["release"] = parts[-3]
            metadata["mno"] = parts[-4].upper()

    return metadata

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""CLI entry point for document content extraction.

Usage:
    # Extract a single document
    python -m src.extraction.extract data/raw/vzw/2026_feb/requirements/LTEDATARETRY.pdf

    # Extract all documents in a directory
    python -m src.extraction.extract data/raw/vzw/2026_feb/requirements/

    # Specify output directory
    python -m src.extraction.extract data/raw/vzw/2026_feb/requirements/ --output data/extracted
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import logging
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

from src.extraction.registry import extract_document, infer_metadata_from_path

SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = {".pdf", ".doc", ".docx", ".xls", ".xlsx"}


def extract_file(file_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> Path | None:
    """Extract a single file and save the result as JSON."""
    metadata = infer_metadata_from_path(file_path)
    logging.info(
        f"Metadata from path: mno={metadata['mno']}, "
        f"release={metadata['release']}, doc_type={metadata['doc_type']}"
    )

    try:
        ir = extract_document(
            file_path,
            mno=metadata["mno"],
            release=metadata["release"],
            doc_type=metadata["doc_type"],
        )
    except ValueError as e:
        logging.error(f"Skipping {file_path.name}: {e}")
        return None

    output_path = output_dir / f"{file_path.stem}_ir.json"
    ir.save_json(output_path)
    logging.info(f"Saved: {output_path} ({ir.block_count} blocks)")
    return output_path


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Extract document content into normalized intermediate representation."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "input",
        type=Path,
        help="Path to a document file or directory of documents.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        type=Path,
        default=Path("data/extracted"),
        help="Output directory for extracted JSON files. Default: data/extracted",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--verbose", "-v", action="store_true", help="Enable verbose logging."
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else logging.INFO,
        format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s",
    )

    input_path: Path = args.input
    output_dir: Path = args.output
    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Collect files to process
    if input_path.is_file():
        files = [input_path]
    elif input_path.is_dir():
        files = sorted(
            f
            for f in input_path.iterdir()
            if f.is_file() and f.suffix.lower() in SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS
        )
    else:
        logging.error(f"Path does not exist: {input_path}")
        sys.exit(1)

    if not files:
        logging.error(f"No supported files found in {input_path}")
        sys.exit(1)

    logging.info(f"Found {len(files)} file(s) to extract")

    start = time.time()
    results = []
    for file_path in files:
        t0 = time.time()
        output_path = extract_file(file_path, output_dir)
        elapsed = time.time() - t0
        if output_path:
            results.append((file_path.name, output_path, elapsed))
            logging.info(f"  {file_path.name}: {elapsed:.1f}s")

    total_elapsed = time.time() - start
    logging.info(f"\nExtraction complete: {len(results)}/{len(files)} files in {total_elapsed:.1f}s")
    for name, out, t in results:
        logging.info(f"  {name} -> {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
All five fixes verified:

1. **Resource leak** — fixed (try/finally around fitz_doc and plumber_pdf)
2. **Hardcoded VZ_REQ** — cross-ref pattern now flows from `_detect_requirement_ids` into `_detect_cross_references` in the profiler, and the parser uses the profile's `components` config to extract plan IDs from req IDs
3. **Trailing dot** — fixed (`\d[\d.]*\d` instead of `[\d.]+`), confirmed no trailing dots in output
4. **SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS mismatch** — CLI now reads from registry via `supported_extensions()`
5. **Profiler header/footer** — now reads from extractor's `extraction_metadata` instead of trying to re-detect from already-filtered IR. Note the output now shows "1 header" pattern (was previously 0 because the old code was searching filtered blocks)

Also fixed the minor dead code (`metadata: dict = {}` and unnecessary `block_type` intermediate variable in `_extract_impl`).

message.content: What do you recommend next?

content.text:
Here's how I see the priorities, in order:

**1. Tests** — This is the biggest gap. We just made 5 changes across 3 modules and verified with a single manual smoke test. That's not sustainable. Before adding more features, I'd write:
- Data model round-trip tests (serialize → deserialize `DocumentIR`, `DocumentProfile`)
- Regex unit tests for the patterns that matter most (section numbering, req ID extraction, 3GPP spec parsing with trailing dots, plan ID extraction from req IDs)
- One pipeline smoke test that runs extract → profile → parse on a real PDF and asserts key properties (block count > 0, plan_id extracted, no trailing-dot specs, etc.)

This is maybe 30 minutes of work and it protects everything we've already built.

**2. Re-generate outputs** — The existing JSON files in `data/extracted/`, `profiles/`, and `data/parsed/` were generated before our fixes. They contain stale data (trailing-dot specs, empty header/footer patterns, etc.). Once tests are in place, re-run the pipeline and commit clean outputs.

**3. Decide the next PoC step** — Looking at the TDD and where we are, the natural next moves are:
- DOCX/XLSX extractors (the TDD lists them as supported formats, and the architecture is ready — just register new extractors)
- Or skip ahead toward the knowledge graph / RAG layer if the VZW PDFs are the only docs you have right now

I'd strongly advocate doing #1 before anything else. Every change from here forward gets riskier without tests. What's your preference?

"""Tests for DocumentIR serialize/deserialize round-trip."""

import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from src.models.document import (
    BlockType,
    ContentBlock,
    DocumentIR,
    FontInfo,
    Position,
)


def _make_text_block(page: int, index: int, text: str, size: float = 12.0) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.PARAGRAPH,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=(72.0, 100.0, 540.0, 120.0)),
        text=text,
        font_info=FontInfo(size=size, bold=False, font_name="TestFont"),
    )


def _make_table_block(page: int, index: int) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.TABLE,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=(72.0, 200.0, 540.0, 400.0)),
        headers=["Col A", "Col B"],
        rows=[["r1c1", "r1c2"], ["r2c1", "r2c2"]],
    )


def _make_image_block(page: int, index: int) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.IMAGE,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=None),
        image_path="images/test.png",
        surrounding_text="Some context",
    )


def _make_ir() -> DocumentIR:
    return DocumentIR(
        source_file="TEST.pdf",
        source_format="pdf",
        mno="VZW",
        release="2026_feb",
        doc_type="requirement",
        content_blocks=[
            _make_text_block(1, 0, "Section heading", size=14.0),
            _make_text_block(1, 1, "Body paragraph with enough text to be meaningful."),
            _make_table_block(1, 2),
            _make_image_block(2, 3),
        ],
        extraction_metadata={"page_count": 2, "header_footer_patterns": ["Pattern #"]},
    )


class TestDocumentIRRoundTrip:
    def test_to_dict_returns_serializable(self):
        ir = _make_ir()
        d = ir.to_dict()
        # Should be JSON-serializable without errors
        json_str = json.dumps(d)
        assert len(json_str) > 0

    def test_round_trip_via_json(self, tmp_path: Path):
        original = _make_ir()
        json_path = tmp_path / "test_ir.json"
        original.save_json(json_path)

        loaded = DocumentIR.load_json(json_path)

        assert loaded.source_file == original.source_file
        assert loaded.source_format == original.source_format
        assert loaded.mno == original.mno
        assert loaded.release == original.release
        assert loaded.doc_type == original.doc_type
        assert loaded.block_count == original.block_count

    def test_round_trip_preserves_text_blocks(self, tmp_path: Path):
        original = _make_ir()
        json_path = tmp_path / "test_ir.json"
        original.save_json(json_path)
        loaded = DocumentIR.load_json(json_path)

        text_blocks = loaded.blocks_by_type(BlockType.PARAGRAPH)
        assert len(text_blocks) == 2
        assert text_blocks[0].text == "Section heading"
        assert text_blocks[0].font_info is not None
        assert text_blocks[0].font_info.size == 14.0
        assert text_blocks[0].font_info.font_name == "TestFont"

    def test_round_trip_preserves_table_blocks(self, tmp_path: Path):
        original = _make_ir()
        json_path = tmp_path / "test_ir.json"
        original.save_json(json_path)
        loaded = DocumentIR.load_json(json_path)

        tables = loaded.blocks_by_type(BlockType.TABLE)
        assert len(tables) == 1
        assert tables[0].headers == ["Col A", "Col B"]
        assert tables[0].rows == [["r1c1", "r1c2"], ["r2c1", "r2c2"]]

    def test_round_trip_preserves_image_blocks(self, tmp_path: Path):
        original = _make_ir()
        json_path = tmp_path / "test_ir.json"
        original.save_json(json_path)
        loaded = DocumentIR.load_json(json_path)

        images = loaded.blocks_by_type(BlockType.IMAGE)
        assert len(images) == 1
        assert images[0].image_path == "images/test.png"
        assert images[0].surrounding_text == "Some context"
        assert images[0].position.bbox is None

    def test_round_trip_preserves_positions(self, tmp_path: Path):
        original = _make_ir()
        json_path = tmp_path / "test_ir.json"
        original.save_json(json_path)

"""Tests for DocumentIR serialize/deserialize round-trip."""

import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from src.models.document import (
    BlockType,
    ContentBlock,
    DocumentIR,
    FontInfo,
    Position,
)


def _make_text_block(page: int, index: int, text: str, size: float = 12.0) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.PARAGRAPH,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=(72.0, 100.0, 540.0, 120.0)),
        text=text,
        font_info=FontInfo(size=size, bold=False, font_name="TestFont"),
    )


def _make_table_block(page: int, index: int) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.TABLE,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=(72.0, 200.0, 540.0, 400.0)),
        headers=["Col A", "Col B"],
        rows=[["r1c1", "r1c2"], ["r2c1", "r2c2"]],
    )


def _make_image_block(page: int, index: int) -> ContentBlock:
    return ContentBlock(
        type=BlockType.IMAGE,
        position=Position(page=page, index=index, bbox=None),
        image_path="images/test.png",
        surrounding_text="Some context",
    )


def _make_ir() -> DocumentIR:
    return DocumentIR(
        source_file="TEST.pdf",
        source_format="pdf",
        mno="VZW",
        release="2026_feb",
        doc_type="requirement",
        content_blocks=[
            _make_text_block(1, 0, "Section heading", size=14.0),
            _make_text_block(1, 1, "Body paragraph with enough text to be meaningful."),
            _make_table_block(1, 2),
            _make_image_block(2, 3),
        ],
        extraction_metadata={"page_count": 2, "header_footer_patterns": ["Pattern #"]},
    )


class TestDocumentIRRoundTrip:
    def test_to_dict_returns_serializable(self):
        ir = _make_ir()
        d = ir.to_dict()
        # Should be JSON-serializable without errors
        json_str = json.dumps(d)
        assert len(json_str) > 0

    def test_round_trip_via_json(self, tmp_path: Path):

"""Normalized intermediate representation for extracted documents.

Matches the schema defined in TDD Section 5.1.7. All format-specific
extractors produce this common representation, consumed uniformly by
the DocumentProfiler and structural parsers.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


class BlockType(str, Enum):
    HEADING = "heading"
    PARAGRAPH = "paragraph"
    TABLE = "table"
    IMAGE = "image"
    EMBEDDED_OBJECT = "embedded_object"


@dataclass
class Position:
    """Location of a content block within the source document."""
    page: int
    index: int  # sequential index across the whole document
    bbox: tuple[float, float, float, float] | None = None  # (x0, y0, x1, y1)


@dataclass
class FontInfo:
    """Font attributes for text-based content blocks.

    Critical for the DocumentProfiler's heading detection — it clusters
    blocks by font size/boldness to derive heading level rules.
    """
    size: float
    bold: bool = False
    italic: bool = False
    font_name: str = ""
    all_caps: bool = False
    color: int = 0  # RGB as integer


@dataclass
class ContentBlock:
    """A single content block in the normalized intermediate representation."""
    type: BlockType
    position: Position

    # Text content (for heading, paragraph)
    text: str = ""
    level: int | None = None  # heading level (from DOCX styles; None for PDF)
    font_info: FontInfo | None = None  # primary font of the block (PDF extraction)
    style: str = ""  # DOCX style name if available

    # Table content
    headers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    rows: list[list[str]] = field(default_factory=list)

    # Image content
    image_path: str = ""
    surrounding_text: str = ""

    # Embedded object content
    object_type: str = ""  # "xlsx", "docx", "pdf", etc.
    extracted_path: str = ""
    extracted_content: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    # Metadata hints for downstream processing
    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class DocumentIR:
    """Normalized intermediate representation for a single document.

    This is the output of the extraction layer and input to both
    the DocumentProfiler and the structural parser.
    """
    source_file: str
    source_format: str  # "pdf", "doc", "docx", "xls", "xlsx"
    mno: str = ""
    release: str = ""
    doc_type: str = ""  # "requirement", "testcase"
    content_blocks: list[ContentBlock] = field(default_factory=list)
    extraction_metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    @property
    def page_count(self) -> int:
        if not self.content_blocks:
            return 0
        return max(b.position.page for b in self.content_blocks)

    @property
    def block_count(self) -> int:
        return len(self.content_blocks)

    def blocks_by_type(self, block_type: BlockType) -> list[ContentBlock]:
        return [b for b in self.content_blocks if b.type == block_type]

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Serialize to a JSON-compatible dict."""
        return asdict(self)

    def save_json(self, path: Path) -> None:
        """Save the IR to a JSON file."""
        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(self.to_dict(), f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

    @classmethod
    def load_json(cls, path: Path) -> DocumentIR:
        """Load an IR from a JSON file."""
        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        blocks = []
        for b in data.get("content_blocks", []):
            pos = Position(**b["position"])
            font = FontInfo(**b["font_info"]) if b.get("font_info") else None
            blocks.append(ContentBlock(
                type=BlockType(b["type"]),
                position=pos,
                text=b.get("text", ""),
                level=b.get("level"),
                font_info=font,
                style=b.get("style", ""),
                headers=b.get("headers", []),
                rows=b.get("rows", []),
                image_path=b.get("image_path", ""),
                surrounding_text=b.get("surrounding_text", ""),
                object_type=b.get("object_type", ""),
                extracted_path=b.get("extracted_path", ""),
                extracted_content=b.get("extracted_content", {}),
                metadata=b.get("metadata", {}),
            ))
        return cls(
            source_file=data["source_file"],
            source_format=data["source_format"],
            mno=data.get("mno", ""),
            release=data.get("release", ""),
            doc_type=data.get("doc_type", ""),
            content_blocks=blocks,
            extraction_metadata=data.get("extraction_metadata", {}),
        )

lines: +                bbox=tuple(bbox_raw) if bbox_raw is not None else None,

"""Tests for DocumentProfile serialize/deserialize round-trip."""

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from src.profiler.profile_schema import (
    BodyText,
    CrossReferencePatterns,
    DocumentProfile,
    DocumentZone,
    HeaderFooter,
    HeadingDetection,
    HeadingLevel,
    MetadataField,
    PlanMetadata,
    RequirementIdPattern,
)


def _make_profile() -> DocumentProfile:
    return DocumentProfile(
        profile_name="test_profile",
        profile_version=2,
        created_from=["DOC_A.pdf", "DOC_B.pdf"],
        last_updated="2026-04-13",
        heading_detection=HeadingDetection(
            method="font_size_clustering",
            levels=[
                HeadingLevel(
                    level=1, font_size_min=13.5, font_size_max=14.5,
                    bold=True, all_caps=True,
                    sample_texts=["1.1 INTRODUCTION"], count=50,
                ),
                HeadingLevel(
                    level=2, font_size_min=13.5, font_size_max=14.5,
                    bold=None, all_caps=True,
                    sample_texts=["1.1.1 APPLICABILITY"], count=10,
                ),
            ],
            numbering_pattern=r"^(\d+\.)+\d*\s",
            max_observed_depth=5,
        ),
        requirement_id=RequirementIdPattern(
            pattern=r"VZ_REQ_[A-Z0-9_]+_\d+",
            components={"prefix": "VZ_REQ", "separator": "_", "plan_id_position": 2},
            sample_ids=["VZ_REQ_TEST_100", "VZ_REQ_TEST_200"],
            total_found=500,
        ),
        plan_metadata=PlanMetadata(
            plan_name=MetadataField(location="first_page", pattern=r"Plan\s+Name:\s*(.+)", sample_value="Test_Plan"),
            plan_id=MetadataField(location="first_page", pattern=r"Plan\s+Id:\s*(\w+)", sample_value="TESTPLAN"),
            version=MetadataField(location="first_page", pattern=r"Version:\s*([\d.]+)", sample_value="5"),
            release_date=MetadataField(),
        ),
        document_zones=[
            DocumentZone(section_pattern=r"^1\.1\b", zone_type="introduction", heading_text="INTRODUCTION"),
            DocumentZone(section_pattern=r"^1\.3\b", zone_type="software_specs", heading_text="SOFTWARE"),
        ],
        header_footer=HeaderFooter(
            header_patterns=["Test Header #"],
            footer_patterns=[],
            page_number_pattern=r"^\s*Page\s+\d+\s+of\s+\d+\s*$",
        ),
        cross_reference_patterns=CrossReferencePatterns(
            standards_citations=[r"3GPP\s+TS\s+[\d.]+"],
